# Vigil — Autonomous Email Agent
# https://vigil.run

> Forward emails to an AI agent. It reads them, tracks conversations, remembers context, and acts when something needs attention. No inbox access. No stored email bodies.

## Quick Start

curl -s https://vigil.run/vigil.sh -o vigil.sh && chmod +x vigil.sh
# Edit: set VK=<api-key> and WATCHER=<watcher-id>
./vigil.sh status

## API

Base: https://api.vigil.run/api
Auth: Authorization: Bearer vk_<key>

GET  /watchers                    — list watchers
POST /watchers                    — create { name, system_prompt }
GET  /watchers/:id/threads        — email threads
POST /watchers/:id/invoke         — chat { message: "..." }
GET  /watchers/:id/memory         — agent memories
GET  /watchers/:id/actions        — action log
GET  /usage                       — cost + usage

## Files

- https://vigil.run/agent-setup.md — agent integration guide
- https://vigil.run/SKILL.md       — agent skill definition
- https://vigil.run/vigil.sh       — CLI wrapper script
- https://vigil.run/llms.txt       — this file

## Docs

- https://vigil.run/learn/integrations  — API + framework examples
- https://vigil.run/learn/watchers      — watcher configuration
- https://vigil.run/learn/agent         — how the agent works
- https://vigil.run/learn/memory        — memory system
- https://vigil.run/learn/architecture  — data flow + privacy

## Source

https://github.com/raulduk3/vigil (BSL 1.1)
